The Accounting Students’ Association (ASA) strives to become the leading student-run association in preparing students for their accounting and business related careers.
ASA was founded in 2001 in conjunction with the Department of Accounting and Business Information Systems at the University of Melbourne. Currently, ASA is a Faculty of Business and Economics (FBE) and University of Melbourne Student Union (UMSU) affiliated student society.
At our events and activities we:
Foster stronger relationships between students and potential employers
Educate Commerce students about the challenging and dynamic opportunities of a career as a qualified accountant
Provide a networking platform between members, their peers and industry leaders
Tailor professional events to suit the diverse range of students at the University of Melbourne
Support the personal and professional development of our members
Share our knowledge and resources to instill University of Melbourne students with valuable transferable skills
Provide personalised events to help our members achieve their career goals
Showcase high quality members to our sponsors.
Club Aims:
Foster stronger relationships between students and potential employers
Educate commerce students about the challenging and dynamic opportunities of a career as a qualified accountant
Provide sponsors with increased exposure via organised functions and advertising mediums
Provide employer organisations with the opportunities of informing students of the qualities and skills they seek from their employees

The Actuarial Students’ Society aims to continuously add value to both our sponsors and our members. To achieve our mission, the Society organises a wide variety of events, in particular, our Sponsors’ Development Workshops, Information Sessions and Contact Night. In addition, our annual publication Vision contains profiles of many Actuarial employers, and we keep our members up to date with industry opportunities via Facebook and e-mails.

ARES
is the Aerospace & Rocket Engineering Society at the University of Melbourne. Through its rocketry and rover teams

enters projects into competitions on behalf of the University, giving students an opportunity to get hands-on as they develop and build rockets and rovers from scratch.

data.desc_full:
Embrace Education
is a non-profit, university student-run organisation that offers free educational support to disadvantaged high school students in Melbourne, Victoria. We help connect club members to high school students who are in need of tutoring and mentoring. We believe that education should be accessible to everyone, regardless of their background. This organisation believes in eradicating education inequality and advocates wholeheartedly for, not only education for all, but also those most impacted by this social injustice, such as marginalised communities and peoples.
The University of Melbourne Chapter
became associated with UMSU in 2017. It has since grown to a beautiful community of like-minded tutors and non-tutors, who wish to make a difference to the world. Our student club aims to support our tutors and students we help and advocate for. It also aims to create events which build a sense of community and instil passion for education to its members and to the wider community. We believe in lifelong learning and the gift of community wisdom and knowledge.

data.desc_short:
Music, Friends, Charity!

Come join our ensembles: Guitar ensemble, Choir, Acapollo (a capella), Rock Bands, Fusion Stage Band, Jazz Band, Strings Ensemble, Composition Workshop, and Jam Sessions!
data.desc_full:
Please note that the first two weeks of rehearsals each semester are free for new members to trial!
With over 250 members across our 13 bands and groups, Apollo Music Society provides the largest student-run music program at The University of Melbourne. Since our humble beginnings as a small choir and string orchestra established in 2002, Apollo has flourished to offer a diverse line-up of opportunities for musicians of all abilities.
Our main ensembles are:
Choir:
This non-auditioned group of singers transcends boundaries and spreads the beauty of choral music. From soaring ballads to catchy show tunes, they embrace diverse genres. Open to singers of all levels, beginners welcome!
Acapollo:
Apollo's a cappella group! We rehearse mainly contemporary pieces like pop, rock, and theatre, with an emphasis on social connection. Open to singers of all levels, beginners welcome!
Strings Ensemble
: A string ensemble specialising in chamber music, as well as modern compositions. Our recent repertoire includes the five variants of "Dives and Lazarus" and an arrangement of the Beatles' "Eleanor Rigby". Open to string players of all levels!
Rock Bands
: Apollo has FOUR rock bands, chill groups that like to have fun and play all types of rock music. Spots are very limited, please follow and reach out on our socials for more information!
Fusion Concert Band
: Any (and we mean ANY) instruments are welcome in this eclectic band! We play contemporary tunes, pop songs and film soundtracks, and our flexible arrangements can accommodate all players! Please reach out to us on our socials for more information!
Guitar Ensemble
: An acoustic guitar band that plays a variety of songs from pop and fingerstyle to classical! Open to guitarists of all levels, beginners welcome!
Music Composition Class
: Compose and produce! Learn and practice your music theory, composing skills, and DAW techniques. Explore diverse styles and collaborate with other passionate creators. Perfect for any music creator: aspiring or affluent in knowledge!
Jazz Band
: It's in their name! Jazz band loves to perform all kinds of jazzy tunes from classic jazz standards to swingin' big band hits, with a focus on music that brings the soul alive! Instrument level requirements will vary depending on the instrument. Please reach out to us on our socials for more information!
Jam sessions
: We host weekly jam sessions open to all of our members, split into two streams: rock and jazz. Express yourself musically and meet new people!
Rehearsal schedules can be found on
our Instagram page
and our weekly bulletins.
What do we do?
We run ten instrumental and choral ensembles, two jam sessions and a music composition workshop every week of the semester. We also have a variety of social events throughout the year such as BBQs, board game nights, trivia and karaoke, but most memorably, our semesterly camps! To top it all off, our semesterly performances - Open Mic, Rock Night, and Charity Concert provide a platform for our talented musicians to perform in front of a supportive audience! All up, we run almost 300 events in a year - woah!
Apollo Music Society is guided by our motto of
'Music, Friends, Charity.'
MUSIC:
Our wide variety of ensembles offer students and other members of the community the opportunity to participate in group music making, weaving beautiful melodies and making lifelong...
FRIENDS:
Our ensembles and social events are the perfect place to meet new people and build friendships to last long beyond your time at university. We pride ourselves on our welcoming and fun atmosphere to make sure that everyone has a great time! And you'll feel especially good about it because Apollo supports...
CHARITY:
Every semester, we hold a charity concert that not only showcases the musical talent of our members but also raises money for worthy causes and charities. In 2024 we raised over $5000!
Club Aims:
To unite students together in their musical interest and add to the diversity of student life.
To promote great music and live music performances to enhance the university environment
To use musical talent to promote health through music by holding charity concerts and donating to health related causes
To travel to hospitals and nursing homes in metropolitan and rural Victoria to perform.

With no dedicated ceramics department at VCA, the club provides access to hands-on workshops, glaze-making sessions, and technical demonstrations on kiln firing. Glaze Research & Innovation: Explore and develop unique glazes through testing and experimentation, with an emphasis on understanding ceramic chemistry and surface textures.
Artist Engagement & Workshops: Host guest artists, workshops, and lectures to expose students to different ceramic practices and contemporary approaches.
Community & Collaboration: Create a supportive environment for students to collaborate, share ideas, and exhibit work, strengthening the ceramics culture at VCA.
Sustainable & Alternative Practices: Encourage exploration of sustainable and unconventional ceramic techniques, including reclaimed clay, alternative firings, and eco-friendly glaze materials.
Technical & Studio Knowledge: Provide guidance on kiln operation, studio maintenance, and best practices for working with ceramics safely and efficiently.
Exhibitions & Public Engagement: Organize exhibitions, pop-up sales, and community projects to showcase student work and engage with the broader Melbourne arts community.
Interdisciplinary Collaboration: Foster connections with students from other disciplines (sculpture, painting, printmaking, etc.) to explore ceramics in hybrid and experimental ways.

data.keywords: China, Chinese, culture
data.desc_short: The club aims to promote and hence consolidate traditional Chinese Culture within the university. A secondary goal of CCS is to provide opportunities for social interaction and exchange of ideas.
data.desc_full:
The Chinese Culture Society (CCS) aims to promote and hence consolidate traditional Chinese Culture within the university. A secondary goal of CCS is to act as a focal point, both social and cultural for students. In addition, CCS provides opportunities for debate on issues that will shape Chinese Culture in the future. CCS has been the heart of the Chinese Groups (C-Groups), which is an alliance of organisations. To extend this aim further, CCS therefore should also act as the representative body for Chinese students (both international and local) both within and outside the university. This means specifically to promote an ethos of forging links with organisations with similar ends. To achieve these ends, the club aims to: Organise events that can both incite interest in the student body and promote and expose participants to Chinese Culture. Encourage participation in these events. Make university life more colourful for all through our presence and participation in university life. Present a welcoming front to students, and to cater for their needs in a social and cultural sense by organising events or running programs of interest to them. Establish cordial relations with other international and cultural groups within university, and in other universities. Establish good relations with synergetic organisations outside of uni, with the aim of allowing members access to benefits provided by these organisations.
In the spirit of reconciliation, the University of Melbourne Chinese Culture Society acknowledges the Traditional Custodians of the country on which the society carries out its works and activities, the Wurundjeri people of the Kulin nation as well as Traditional Custodians of country throughout Australia and their connections to land, sea and community. We pay our respect to elders past and present and extend that respect to all Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander. We sincerely hope the works and activities of the society can contribute to the important processes of reconciliation and cultural exchange.
Club Aims:
To promote understanding of and hence consolidate traditional Chinese Culture.
To promote understanding of and hence consolidate modern Chinese Culture in its relevant contemporary context.
To provide opportunities for debate on issues that will shape Chinese Culture in future.
To provide opportunities for social interaction and exchange of ideas among:
Members of this Society;
Members of this Society and the community.
Chinese Culture Society

data.keywords: altruism, change, charity, effective, empathy, good, impact, networking, projects, social
data.desc_short: We are dedicated towards answering the question, "using evidence and reason, how can I do the most good possible?" We run educational courses, education events, career events, and regular socials.
data.desc_full:
Who we are:
We are a campus group aiming to spread the ideas of Effective Altruism. Effective altruism is a philosophy and social movement that applies evidence and reasoning to the question, ‘using the limited resources that I have, how can I do the most good?’
Values:
Global empathy: The belief that all lives have equal value, regardless of their distance from us or their race, gender, sexuality or ability.
Cause neutrality: Rather than doing what feels right, we aim to consider all causes and actions, and then act in the way that brings about the greatest positive impact.
Cause prioritization: We aim to use evidence and reasoning to prioritise causes based on importance, neglectedness and tractability. Some popular cause areas in the Effective Altruism community include global poverty, climate change, existential risks and animal suffering.
Read more here:
–
https://www.effectivealtruism.org/
–
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Effective_altruism
–
https://eaunimelb.com/
Feel free to contact us with any questions you might have. We value different opinions and perspectives, and are dedicated to facilitating respectful discussion.
Club Aims
To promote the ideas of the Effective Altruism movement to University of Melbourne students.
To support Effective Altruists on campus by facilitating networking, individual projects, and co-working sessions.
To raise money for effective charities using established Effective Altruism principles.
To educate the community regarding emerging issues relevant to Effective Altruism.
To encourage the broader application of Effective Altruism principles by running regular events.
Effective Altruism

About the Society:
The Veterinary Students Society of Victoria (VSSV) is committed to fostering connections among veterinary students, faculty, and industry professionals through the organisation of social events and educational activities, while also serving as advocates for student concerns. The society is comprised of two committees, located in Parkville and Werribee, representing DVM1-2 and DVM3-4 students, respectively. These committees collaborate to fulfill the mission of the VSSV.
Beyond facilitating peer interactions, the VSSV serves as the voice of the student body in discussions with faculty and acts as a liaison between the industry and students, striving to ensure the highest standards of veterinary education and industry exposure. The VSSV's mission is further supported by the Faculty of Science, the
University of Melbourne Student Union (UMSU)
, the
Graduate Student Association (GSA)
, and various industry sponsors.
Throughout the year, the VSSV organises numerous social events, including the Vet Ball, Barn Dance, Networking Dinner, and Halfway Dinner, along with various smaller social, sports, and well-being activities. A key benefit of the VSSV for our members is through offering discounts on branded merchandise. Our merchandise includes apparel for placements, professional polo shirts, and dissection kits, all designed to support students throughout their veterinary education
Our Special Interest Groups (SIGs) offer students the chance to delve into their specific interests, whether related to small animals, large animals, wildlife, exotics, or animal welfare. Each group organises events throughout the year and operates its own committees and meetings.
Society Aims:
To provide a society to unify the students in the first and second years of the Veterinary Science course.
To act as a communication link between the students and the lecturers and staff of the Veterinary Science Faculty.
To facilitate the opportunity for Veterinary students to gain contacts and network with the Veterinary Industry.
To encourage the field of veterinary science through the dissemination of veterinary information and knowledge.

data.desc_full:
We are the student and youth movement of the world Trotskyist movement. All the great problems confronting humanity in the 21st century -- war, poverty, climate change, authoritarianism -- are the outcome of capitalism and can only be addressed through socialist revolution led by the world working class.
To fight for socialism today, young people must understand the history of struggles of the working class in the 20th century. To build a global movement against imperialist war, the IYSSE is educating youth in the history and principles of Trotskyism, the Marxism of the 21st century.
Aims:
As the student and youth movement of the Socialist Equality Party, the Australian section of the world Trotskyist movement, the International Committee of the Fourth International (ICFI), the IYSSE aims to educate students in the program and principles of the ICFI.
The IYSSE seeks to develop an international anti-war movement among students and young people, that will fight to mobilise the working class in Australia and throughout the Asia-Pacific region in a unified struggle against the US-led pivot to Asia and the preparations for war against China.
The IYSSE will provide students with an understanding of the lessons of the strategic historical experiences of the working class throughout the 20th Century, most importantly, the political and theoretical struggles against those tendencies that broke from the Fourth International and then the ICFI on the basis of a rejection of its program and principles.
To promote the intellectual traditions of classical Marxism associated with the names of Marx, Engels, Lenin, Luxemburg and Trotsky, against the theories of the Frankfurt School, postmodernism and identity politics.
To sponsor regular forums discussing the daily political analysis of the

, the Internet site of the ICFI, dealing with Marxist philosophy, global politics, economics, history, science and the arts.

nodes.id: Htet Htet Aung Wang Yi Htin
Networking for Ecological and Marine Opportunities (NEMO)

data.desc_full:
Do you love the ocean? Are you interested in your future career? Do you want to get the know your cohort a bit better? If you answered yes to any of these questions, you should join NEMO!
Networking for Ecological and Marine Opportunities (NEMO) is a club for anyone interested in marine science, ecology, biodiversity, networking opportunities and having fun. We aim to create a community with similar interests and the same love of the ocean and all its components.
Our events range from expert talks from within our university and networking with industry leaders, to snorkelling and having fun.
Club Aims:
Foster a Collaborative Community - One of our primary goals is to create an inclusive and vibrant community where students passionate about marine biology, the ocean, and Ecology can come together, share ideas, and collaborate. By providing a platform for students to connect, exchange knowledge, and discuss their common interests, we aim to cultivate an environment where students can feel supported and inspired to pursue their passion for marine science. Whether you're an undergraduate student or a master's candidate, NEMO will serve as a space for everyone to engage in meaningful interactions with their peers and faculty alike.
Provide Networking Opportunities with Experts - Through monthly events and guest speaker sessions, NEMO aims to bridge the gap between students and established professionals in marine biology, ecology, and related fields. By inviting professors, researchers, and industry leaders to share their experiences and insights, we provide students with invaluable networking opportunities to learn directly from those shaping the future of marine science and Ecology. These connections will help open doors for career opportunities, internships, and collaborative research initiatives.
Encourage Hands-On Learning and Field Experiences - We believe that some of the best learning happens outside the classroom, which is why NEMO is dedicated to organising field trips, snorkel and surf outings, and other hands-on experiences. These activities allow students to apply their academic knowledge in real-world settings while also building teamwork skills and fostering a deeper appreciation for marine ecosystems. From exploring local marine reserves to attending industry-related field trips, these experiences provide unique insights into marine science careers.
Promote Research Collaboration and Volunteer Opportunities - NEMO aims to be a platform for students to engage in meaningful research projects by connecting them with PhD students and faculty conducting cutting-edge marine biology research. Whether students are interested in volunteering as research assistants or participating in ongoing studies, our club provides the bridge to these opportunities. By encouraging collaboration between undergraduates and advanced researchers, NEMO helps foster the next generation of marine scientists and ecologists and ensures that students gain real-world experience and contribute to important ecological studies.
Support Career and Academic Development - Navigating the transition from undergraduate studies to graduate school or industry roles can be daunting. NEMO is committed to helping students explore various pathways in marine science, from graduate programs to diverse career roles in both academia and the private sector. Through workshops, panels, and one-on-one discussions, we aim to equip our members with the guidance and tools they need to make informed decisions about their future in marine biology, whether that involves pursuing a master's degree, finding a research assistantship, or stepping into a professional role.

What is Overseas Christian Fellowship (OCF)?
O is for Overseas:
OCF Melbourne Uni is a student-led, interdenominational Christian club catered to international students in the University of Melbourne, but we do have local students in the club as well.
C is for Christian:
We believe in Jesus Christ and His Word, so we gather every Friday night for a time of prayer, worship, and cell group bible studies. If you are still exploring the faith and want to ask the hard questions about Christianity, we also have Discovery Group.
F is for Fellowship:
This is a home away from home where you can grow in a Christian community. We hold many events and activities throughout the year, including cell retreats, camps, sports and various training workshops to help you know God better. We also reach our campus by giving out coffee during exam weeks and hotcross buns during Easter. There are many opportunities to serve and further develop your gifts with our various ministries: worship, prayer, outreach, cell groups, BUM (Build Up Ministry), admin.
Whoever you are and wherever you are from, if you want your university experience to be life-changing, join us!

Time: Fridays, 6:15 pm Venue: Arts West Forum Theatre (Check our Instagram page for updates)

The Platypus Affiliated Society organises reading groups, public fora, research and journalism focused on the problems and tasks inherited from the “Old” (1920s-30s), “New” (1960s-70s) and post-political (1980s-90s) Left for the possibilities of emancipatory politics today.
The aims of the club are:
To host public fora on the history and contemporary currents of Left political thought for student audiences, featuring a purposefully diverse range of opinions from its different intellectual traditions.
To host recurring, year-long reading groups on the history of Left philosophical and political thought from 1755 to 1969, covering thinkers from the Enlightenment such as Jean-Jacques Rousseau and Adam Smith, the Socialist thought of Karl Marx and Friedrich Engels, and the critical theory of the Frankfurt School.
To conduct interviews with the notable thinkers of the Left today for publication in our free international monthly journal, The Platypus Review.
To have regular on-campus casual discussion groups – ‘coffee breaks’ – where students can come and openly discuss the panels that we’ve hosted, along with all aspects of their experiences with political thought and activity on the left.
To host movie screenings, covering a wide selection of historical documentaries and biographical films of political characters from the Left.
To enrich campus life with an intellectual forum for discussing Left history and political theory, without the constraints or added pressure of student activism.

data.keywords: manage, management, marketing, networking, SAMM, students
data.desc_short: SAMM is a student society that aims to bridge the gap between students and the management & marketing industries, offering a wide range of networking events and opportunities, and skills workshops
data.desc_full:
What is SAMM? SAMM is a thriving student network that provides Management and Marketing undergraduate and postgraduate students at the University of Melbourne with opportunities to extend student learning beyond the classroom. Our vision: “Be prepared, stay informed, get connected” is at the core of everything that we do and all our offerings to our members. We enable and enrich our students to become prepared through building their professional skills, to stay informed with valuable industry insight and to build meaningful connections with industry professionals.
What does our vision mean?
BE PREPARED
Equipping you with skills necessary in ascertaining that grad role and succeeding on the job, SAMM aims to have all our members prepared for when they graduate university. Through our professional development workshops and case competitions, build skills that you won’t learn in the clasroom such as how to perfect your resume and interview responses and how to create a business presentation.
STAY INFORMED
Stay informed with the industry trends in Marketing and Management. SAMM will enrich your knowledge through our engaging seminars and online content. Through The Uni Diaries, keep up with the scoop, be stimulated by debate on Management, Marketing and culture. We aim to ensure that our members are on top the ever evolving ecosystem of business.
GET CONNECTED
Building business relationships and connecting with other students, professionals in industries and academia are fundamental to future career success. Membership provides you with plenty of opportunities to create networks and friends to find your footing early in your career. Our past flagship events played host to well known and respected companies, with representatives from the likes of L’Oréal, Telstra, BCG, Interbrand, Deloitte, IBM and Mondelez International.
Club Aims:
To organise events which facilitate networking opportunities with industry professionals for the students in hopes of developing their networking and communication skills, crucial in the business sector.
To organise information sessions which aim to bring Melbourne University alumni, Melbourne University staff working within the Careers offices as well as industry professionals who have recently joined the workforce to provide students with relatable pathways and answers to questions on how to pursue a successful pathway in Management and Marketing.
To facilitate a casual environment with social events, dissimilar to our informative, professional events, which primarily aim to assist students with meeting other students studying and wishing to take similar pathways as themselves, in hopes of creating a support web with peers.
To facilitate interactive events such as case competitions and debates which allow students to display their talents to a panel of industry professions, receive real time feedback and, ultimately, allow the occurrence of a “winner” who receives some form of exclusive prize related to Management or Marketing.
To produce a fortnightly newsletter which lists content that has been in relation to Management or Marketing during that period, promote the opportunities and benefits of past and upcoming SAMM events as well as display important future dates relevant to the student such as internship application periods etc.
131 Barry Street, The University of Melbourne
Carlton 3053

data.desc_full:
Thai Student Association (TSA): A Home Away from Home for Thai Students in Melbourne
Established in 1992, the Thai Student Association (TSA) initially served as a community for Thai students at the University of Melbourne. Today, our mission has evolved to promote inclusivity and offer support to both undergraduate and graduate students across various universities in Melbourne, including RMIT, Swinburne, Monash, and others.
We strive to create meaningful connections through a variety of activities, from study sessions and excursions to night markets, movie nights, board games, and picnics. Our community designed these events to help Thai students connect, make new friends and feel like home in Melbourne. Moreover, we welcome the broader student community to immerse themselves into Thai culture; through entertainment, movies, music, and more, fostering a rich environment for cultural exchange. We are dedicated to host events that not only unite Thai students but also create a sense of belonging to the community.
Club Aims:
To create a welcoming and inclusive community for Thai students from universities across Melbourne, providing a strong support and fostering a sense of belonging.
To provide opportunities for Thai students to collaborate, network, and build lasting friendships across different universities.
To support Thai students in navigating life in Melbourne by offering resources, peer support, and shared experiences.
To promote and celebrate Thai culture through engaging activities that bring Thai and non-Thai students together.

nodes.id: University Network for Investing and Trading (UNIT)

data.keywords: finance, Investing, money, stockmarket, Trading
data.desc_short: From our inception in 2007, UNIT has become the largest investing and trading society in Australia. Our national network aims to connect all students to opportunities with peers, alumni and sponsors.
data.desc_full:
Who we are
Since its inception in 2007, the University Network for Investing and Trading (UNIT) has become the largest investing and trading society in Australia with 4,500 student members nationally. UNIT Chapters are located in four major Australian universities; the University of Sydney, the University of New South Wales, Macquarie University and the University of Melbourne. We strive to provide a practical financial toolkit for novices and more sophisticated members through educational seminars, professional opportunities with our sponsors and national competitions across all participating universities. Our major contributions towards member engagement for 2014 include the national Stock Pitch Challenge and the state-organised Women in Finance Evening. You most certainly need not be a finance major to join our club nor appreciate the breadth of our events.
Why join us
While there are many business and finance-related societies at each participating university, UNIT’s unique philosophy sets it apart.
UNIT gives members extensive exposure to the broad field of finance by hosting presentations from industry leaders including; investment banks, investment management firms, proprietary trading firms, commercial banks, stockbrokers, corporate advisors and hedge funds. UNIT endeavours to provide the financial education university classrooms cannot; how to open brokerage accounts, how to use excel shortcuts for financial modelling, how to screen for stocks and how to make sound financial decisions.
We share our knowledge for the sake of your understanding. We do not pretend to know the answers; we merely try to present options from which you can make your own informed financial decisions.
Club Aims:
Increase awareness of different concepts within Personal Finance among the general student populace – specifically Investing, Trading, and other various topics associated with personal finance.
Organize educational workshops to equip members with practical skills not learned in the classroom – such as financial modelling and company valuations, use of the Microsoft Suite, practical investing, and stock pitching.
Inform members about current trends in the markets through a joint newsletter publication with the Macquarie University, University of Sydney, and University of New South Wales.
Connect University of Melbourne members to like-minded peers in partner universities across Australia, in order to increase their network and sharing of information.
Allow University of Melbourne members to apply their theoretical knowledge in a practical manner through investing competitions, and enrich the general university experience.
Engage students from other faculties providing them general knowledge on personal finance and within that; investing and trading.
Use our collective knowledge to improve access to knowledge relating to personal finances in communities to create a social benefit.
University Network for Investing and Trading (UNIT)

data.desc_short: Are you interested in meeting new people, learning new skills, building your resume, and giving back to your community? Join us and be a part of something bigger than yourself.
data.desc_full:
Youth Charity Society (YCS) is a student-led volunteering club enthusiastic about reaching out to isolated individuals in our community, with efforts to encourage more young adults to volunteer. We aim to create a comfortable and welcoming space for youths to share experiences and skills acquired through volunteering with us. This year, we are ready to bring even more fun and meaningful volunteering events back on campus and we can't wait to see you there!
If you're looking to gain leadership experience, build your resume, and make a difference in the world, our volunteering club is for you. We offer a supportive and collaborative environment where you can develop your skills while giving back to your community. Some opportunities that we offer include working with large not-for-profit organisations such as SecondBite and Sacred Heart Mission. We also engage in fundraising, with bake sales being conducted on campus to raise funds for notable organisations such as  Cancer Council Victoria and RMCC. However, this is just the beginning of what Youth Charity Society offers!
Volunteering hours are recorded and
certificates of appreciation
are presented at the end of the year.
What's more? At YCS, we're more than just colleagues or members - we're a family. We host regular fun member bonding events to foster a sense of camaraderie and togetherness among our community. Join us for movie nights, picnics, group study sessions (with bubble tea!) and other activities that will help you get to know your fellow members.
So don’t hesitate, join us to gain valuable experience and opportunities to engage with the community around us!
Club Aims:
To provide a space for compassionate students and volunteers to unite together, socialise and share experiences.
To organise events to stimulate fellow students to volunteer or participate in community work, and also organise fundraiser events.
Link students to Charities: to cooperate with various volunteering and charitable programs to introduce students to wide variety of community services. Students will not be limited to a single organisation but will have a variety of opportunities to choose from.
